jokes.text: Oh, anything that doesn't have the word 'strip,' 'salsa,' or 'beatz' with a Z in the name of it.
jokes.setup: Being asked what fitness class to take
jokes.punchline: Specific aversion to trendy fitness class naming conventions, especially 'beatz' with a Z
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with character-specific neuroses. The Z detail elevates it from generic to specific.

jokes.text: I finally just threw them out this morning 'cause they got that really bad flower smell. I kind of couldn't stop smelling them.
jokes.punchline: Liz's neurotic relationship with the flowers - calling the natural smell 'bad' and admitting compulsive smelling
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Liz's neurotic character - turning a romantic gesture into an obsessive problem.

jokes.text: Don't give up, ponytail.
jokes.punchline: Reducing Liz to just 'ponytail' - impersonal and slightly condescending
jokes.explanation: Economical character moment showing fitness culture's depersonalization.

jokes.text: That is the cutest thing I've ever seen. Isn't that adorable? You have to fire 10% of your staff.
jokes.setup: Jack showing Liz an adorable baby panda video
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to harsh business directive after establishing warm moment
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock misdirection - using cute moment to deliver corporate brutality. Perfect structure and character integration.

jokes.text: We're synergizing backward overflow.
jokes.setup: Need for explanation of corporate policy
jokes.punchline: Meaningless corporate buzzword combination
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate speak satire - sounds important but means nothing.

jokes.text: Now, there are 140 people on this show, so go out there and make 126 people very happy.
jokes.punchline: Reframing mass firings as making the survivors happy
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate logic presented as motivation. Jack's twisted optimism perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Marco. Polo! Marco! Polo! Josh, you suck at this game, man.
jokes.punchline: Cut to writers playing Marco Polo in the office
jokes.explanation: Good visual contradiction to Liz's claim, though fairly obvious workplace absurdism.

jokes.text: Those big farm people hands crushin' my windpipe.
jokes.punchline: Vivid imagery of wife's threatening 'farm people hands'
jokes.explanation: 'Farm people hands' is a brilliantly specific and quotable phrase that captures both fear and class commentary.

jokes.text: You mean that you flat-out lied about getting a vasectomy? Don't tell me that backfired.
jokes.punchline: Liz's sarcastic shock that lying about medical procedures could backfire
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery, though the setup is doing most of the work.

jokes.text: How good, like Judaism good, or just like Unitarian?
jokes.setup: Tracy asking about finding a good church
jokes.punchline: Liz's ranking system for religious quality
jokes.explanation: Clever hierarchy joke that's both economical and character-specific. The casual ranking of religions is perfectly Liz.

jokes.text: My attorney told me I should join a church preemptively. 'Cause juries are suspicious of celebrities who find religion after getting into trouble.
jokes.punchline: Strategic religious conversion for legal purposes
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of celebrity culture and cynical legal strategy. Perfect Tracy logic.

jokes.text: I believe the moon doesn't exist. I believe that vampires are the world's greatest golfers, but their curse is that they'll never get to prove it. I believe that there are 31 letters in the white alphabet.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he was rejected from Scientology
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly bizarre beliefs, ending with 'white alphabet'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdism. Each belief is more ridiculous, with 'white alphabet' being a perfect closer. Peak Tracy Jordan logic.

jokes.text: Mm, I pretty much just do whatever Oprah tells me to.
jokes.punchline: Oprah as religious authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character truth. Captures modern pseudo-spirituality in one line.

jokes.text: You know, you could be pretty if you didn't scowl so much.
jokes.punchline: Immediately inappropriate comment to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Establishes Eddie's inappropriate character but fairly standard creepy guy behavior.

jokes.text: That's Dona-gee, not Dona-hee!
jokes.punchline: Brothers fighting over family name pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag about class differences within Irish family.

jokes.text: What'd you two dirtbags do now? Fix the Cotton Bowl?
jokes.punchline: Assuming elaborate sports gambling scheme
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific assumption that reveals family history of schemes.

jokes.text: I haven't seen Eddie since I bailed him out of Disney jail.
jokes.punchline: The specific concept of 'Disney jail'
jokes.explanation: 'Disney jail' is a perfectly absurd detail that implies a whole backstory. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: My dad and Eddie are a blotch on the Dona-hee name-- Dona-gee name.
jokes.setup: Jack's serious moment about family shame
jokes.punchline: Catching himself using the wrong pronunciation even in emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Good running gag payoff that shows how deep the class anxiety runs.

jokes.text: We haven't been Presbyterian for months. And y'all always meet on Wednesday nights? Yeah, we lose half the congregation every time American Idol starts up.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking about church denomination and schedule
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that American Idol trumps religious devotion
jokes.explanation: Good observation about modern priorities, though not particularly surprising.

jokes.text: He pointed right at me! He sure did!
jokes.punchline: Tracy's paranoid reaction to being singled out, confirmed by congregation
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment but fairly predictable fish-out-of-water reaction.

jokes.text: Banana walnut, your favorite. Uh, that's not correct, but okay.
jokes.setup: Eager subordinate bringing boss her 'favorite' muffin
jokes.punchline: Liz's matter-of-fact correction delivered without emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz deadpan delivery. The politeness combined with correction is very character-specific.

jokes.text: You make smart sexy.
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at complimenting Liz
jokes.explanation: Captures workplace sucking up well but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: I talk homeless people into joining the Army.
jokes.setup: Eddie claiming he has a 'real job' now
jokes.punchline: His job is ethically questionable military recruitment
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific job that perfectly captures Eddie's moral ambiguity while being economical.

jokes.text: Juvie, Jonestown, that time I punched Goofy.
jokes.punchline: Escalation from juvenile detention to cult to Disney violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with 'punched Goofy' as the absurd peak. Excellent economy and callbacks.

jokes.text: I hated that bird.
jokes.setup: Eddie confessing to microwaving Jack's parakeet
jokes.punchline: Jack's understated reaction to pet murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to horrific revelation. The understatement makes it funnier and more character-specific.

jokes.text: No, it's... it's only inappropriate when it's ugly people.
jokes.punchline: Revealing shallow double standard about attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Dark admission that reveals hypocrisy in social norms. Good character moment for Liz.

jokes.text: I know who I can fire!
jokes.setup: Learning Floyd's girlfriend works on the show
jokes.punchline: Immediate scheming to eliminate romantic competition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's capacity for petty scheming when it comes to romance.

jokes.text: Ugh, you can just tell she is by her stupid face!
jokes.punchline: Completely irrational justification based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's pettiness, though fairly standard jealousy humor.

jokes.text: You look like that lady astronaut who tried to kidnap that other woman.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Lisa Nowak astronaut scandal
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that perfectly captures the specific type of unhinged behavior Liz is displaying.

jokes.text: Diapers, Mace, Houston to Orlando in nine hours? Blammo.
jokes.punchline: Admiring the criminal efficiency with 'Blammo'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the astronaut bit with Liz's twisted admiration. 'Blammo' is perfectly quotable.

jokes.text: For the first time ever, things are lining up for old Liz Lemon.
jokes.punchline: The phrase 'old Liz Lemon' referring to herself
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific phrase that becomes quotable, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: But you had to go to the bathroom, so Dad missed seeing Meredith Vieira!
jokes.punchline: Meredith Vieira as the pinnacle of entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about what constitutes a major disappointment for elderly person.

jokes.text: They never once said anything about the racist stuff towards the end.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of father's deathbed racism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family dysfunction. The casual delivery of horrible information is perfectly written.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, we're Irish. We're a forgiving people.
jokes.punchline: Claiming Irish people are forgiving despite centuries of evidence to the contrary
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation about Irish stereotypes that Jack would genuinely believe.

jokes.text: Kabbalah is a wonderful religion that mixes the fun part of Judaism with magic.
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex mysticism to 'fun Judaism plus magic'
jokes.explanation: Good oversimplification that captures celebrity spiritual shopping perfectly.

jokes.text: This one sounds really expensive and gay.
jokes.punchline: Eddie's crude assessment
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but relies on lazy stereotypes without clever insight.

jokes.text: You know, because of The Da Vinci Code.
jokes.punchline: Attributing church problems to Dan Brown novel instead of actual scandals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - audience expects reference to sex abuse scandals but gets harmless fiction reference. Perfect Eddie obliviousness.

jokes.text: I bet Pops is looking down right now and saying, 'If I could come back and do any broad, it'd be that one right there.'
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate sexualization
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that establishes Eddie's complete lack of social awareness, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: That's Patrick, this is Patricia, this is Katherine Catherine, and her husband Bobby, and this is, uh, Margaret. We just found out about her today.
jokes.punchline: The discovery of Margaret as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist family chaos with the casual discovery of a new family member.

jokes.text: Come here, you magnificent douche bag!
jokes.punchline: Affectionate insult combination
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic humor with creative insult-as-endearment.

jokes.text: Stop showing off and have a drink.
jokes.punchline: Treating sobriety as pretentious behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark family dynamic where healthy behavior is seen as showing off. Very economical.

jokes.text: Stop showing off and have a drink.
jokes.punchline: Family treating sobriety as showing off
jokes.explanation: Dark family dysfunction where sobriety is seen as arrogance. Captures toxic family dynamics perfectly with economy.

jokes.text: What would you say are your weaknesses? Some people say I'm too nice.
jokes.punchline: Classic terrible interview answer cliché
jokes.explanation: Recognizable interview cliché but not particularly original or surprising.

jokes.text: You're fired! I'm the decider!
jokes.punchline: 'The decider' reference to Bush presidency
jokes.explanation: Good political reference that fits character moment, though somewhat dated.

jokes.text: You're fired! You're all fired! Clean out your desks! Fired!
jokes.setup: Liz's firing spree getting out of control
jokes.punchline: Complete meltdown with mass firings
jokes.explanation: Good escalation sequence showing complete power corruption, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: And you still think our next President should be a woman?
jokes.setup: Jack reacting to Liz's petty behavior
jokes.punchline: Using her pettiness as argument against female leadership
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - taking personal incident as proof of broader political point. Brilliantly inappropriate logic.

jokes.text: Lemon, you've gone chicken killer on me over a guy whose name you don't know?
jokes.punchline: 'Chicken killer' as metaphor for irrational behavior
jokes.explanation: 'Chicken killer' is a brilliantly specific metaphor that perfectly captures the irrational destructiveness.

jokes.text: Eddie just said to write the initials on the check. C... A... S... H.
jokes.setup: Liz asking how to make out charity check
jokes.punchline: Realizing 'C.A.S.H.' spells exactly what it sounds like
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect setup-payoff structure. The realization builds perfectly.

jokes.text: Dad? Your brother Eddie's dead. He wanted you to have his watch.
jokes.setup: Serious moment of receiving father's watch
jokes.punchline: Father immediately trying same con about Eddie being dead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that reveals the con while showing it's a family tradition. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: It's pronounced Dona-fee, you lace curtain half-an-Englishman!
jokes.punchline: Father has yet another pronunciation and class-based insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of running gag with specific Irish class terminology.

jokes.text: Say hello to Bono and Sandra Day O'Connor!
jokes.setup: Brothers naming their fists for fighting
jokes.punchline: Jack's fist names are pretentious celebrities
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Jack character moment. Naming fists after U2 frontman and Supreme Court Justice is peak pretentious absurdity.

jokes.text: Say hello to Bono and Sandra Day O'Connor!
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of U2 singer and Supreme Court Justice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect character logic - Jack would name his fists after his heroes.

jokes.text: I want you...to punch your sister in the face.
jokes.setup: Family brawl getting out of hand
jokes.punchline: Liz's specific violent request delivered to camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of chaos with Liz's matter-of-fact violence. The direct address makes it funnier.

jokes.text: To know that they'll be there after I'm dead, fighting over my corpse before it's cryogenically frozen.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of cryogenic freezing plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - treating cryogenic freezing as natural life plan. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It seems that things are lining up once again for old Liz Lemon.
jokes.punchline: Return of her catchphrase after getting what she wanted through Jack's intervention
jokes.explanation: Good callback that closes the loop on character catchphrase, though not particularly surprising.

jokes.text: I should've just gotten a vasectomy! Let me explain.
jokes.setup: Lutz mentioning vasectomy secret in front of flower guy
jokes.punchline: Awkward explanation attempt to confused third party
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates new awkward situation. The escalation to involving stranger is appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I should've just gotten a vasectomy!
jokes.punchline: Finally reaching obvious conclusion after elaborate deception
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolution though fairly predictable character realization.

jokes.text: I'm IrisCatholic now. Like you, Regis, and the Pope.
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'Irish Catholic' and bizarre celebrity grouping
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy malapropism with absurd celebrity categorization that fits his worldview.

jokes.text: Whether things are good, or bad, or you're simply eating tacos in the park. There is always the crushing guilt.
jokes.punchline: Even innocuous activities like eating tacos carry guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with absurdly specific detail that captures Catholic guilt perfectly. 'Tacos in the park' is brilliantly mundane.
